title: Top 9 Herbal Remedies for Effective Relief from Excessive Sweating

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Avenanthramides are good for excessive sweating because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the sweat glands, reducing the frequency and intensity of excessive sweating.
Saponins are good for excessive sweating because they have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help to reduce sweat production and prevent bacterial growth on the skin.
Phenolic acids, commonly known as fennel, are good for excessive sweating because they have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce sweat gland activity and improve skin health.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa tincture is a natural remedy that can help with excessive sweating. This is because it contains chemicals that help calm the nervous system, which is often overactive in people who sweat a lot. When the nervous system is calm, it sends less stress signals to the body, which means less sweating. Avena sativa also contains compounds that help regulate hormones and reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to excessive sweating. Taking Avena sativa tincture can help reduce the frequency and amount of sweat, and it can also help alleviate anxiety and stress that often accompany excessive sweating. Avena sativa is also known to balance the body's thermostat, helping to regulate temperature and reduce excessive sweating. Some people also use it to help reduce hot flashes and night sweats, which can be a symptom of menopause or other hormonal imbalances. By calming the nervous system and regulating hormones, Avena sativa tincture can help bring excessive sweating under control.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Foeniculum vulgare herbal tincture that helps with excessive sweating, you'll need dried fennel seeds and a solvent like vodka or glycerin. Start by measuring 250 grams of dried fennel seeds and placing them in a clean glass jar. Next, pour 750 milliliters of vodka or glycerin over the fennel seeds, making sure they're completely covered. Close the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the solvent extract the active compounds from the fennel seeds. After the steeping period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. You'll now have a strong fenniculum vulgare tincture that can be used to help alleviate excessive sweating. To use it, mix a few drops of the tincture with water and apply it to the affected areas, or take it internally as directed by a healthcare professional.
remedies.image_alt: Avena sativa herbal tinctures for excessive sweating

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for excessive sweating because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the production of sweat and also alleviate the symptoms of hyperhidrosis.

Hyperforin has shown to be beneficial in reducing excessive sweating due to its antiseptic properties that help in killing bacteria that cause body odor, thereby promoting a fresher and healthier skin.
Kaempferol is good for excessive sweating because it has antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and soothe the skin, thereby controlling sweat glands and preventing excessive sweating.
Hyperoside is good for excessive sweating because it has been shown to have a cooling effect on the skin and also helps to reduce inflammation, which can contribute to excessive sweating.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum tincture, also known as St. John's Wort, is often used to help manage excessive sweating. This is because it has properties that can help calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety, which are common causes of excessive sweating. When we're stressed or anxious, our body's "fight or flight" response is triggered, leading to increased sweat production. Hypericum perforatum tincture contains compounds like hyperforin and hypericin, which have a soothing effect on the nervous system, helping to calm the body and mind. By reducing anxiety and stress, the tincture can help decrease excessive sweat production, providing relief from symptoms like sweaty palms, underarms, and feet. Additionally, the tincture's anti-inflammatory properties may also help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation caused by excessive sweating.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Foeniculum vulgare, or fennel, herbal tincture that helps with excessive sweating, you'll need dried fennel seeds and a solvent like vodka or glycerin. Start by filling a clean glass jar about 2/3 full with the dried fennel seeds. Next, pour the solvent over the seeds, making sure they're completely covered. The general ratio is 1 part fennel seeds to 2 parts solvent, but you can adjust it to your liking. Secure the lid tightly and store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the solvent extract the herb's properties. After the extraction period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Your fennel tincture is now ready to use, and it can be stored in a cool, dark place for up to a year. To use it, simply add 20-30 drops to water or another liquid and consume it as needed to help manage excessive sweating.
remedies.image_alt: Hypericum perforatum herbal tinctures for excessive sweating

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Glycyrrhizin is good for excessive sweating because it has a cooling effect that helps to reduce body temperature and sweat production, thereby providing relief from excessive sweating.
Flavonoids, or fennel, may be good for excessive sweating because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce sweat gland activity and improve overall skin health.
Polyphenols (Fennel) may help reduce excessive sweating by acting as natural antioxidants, which help calm down the nervous system and regulate body temperature, thereby reducing sweat production.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tincture is a natural remedy that can help with excessive sweating. This is because it contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has antiperspirant properties. Glycyrrhizin helps to stop the nerves from sending signals to the sweat glands, reducing the amount of sweat produced. It also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm the skin. This makes it a great option for people who experience excessive sweating due to anxiety or stress. Additionally, glycyrrhizin has been shown to have a mild diuretic effect, which can help to remove excess fluids from the body. By reducing the amount of sweat produced and helping the body to remove excess fluids, Glycyrrhiza glabra tincture can help to alleviate excessive sweating symptoms.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Foeniculum vulgare herbal tincture that helps with excessive sweating, start by gathering dried fennel seeds, which come from the Foeniculum vulgare plant. You will also need a clean glass jar with a lid, a cutting tool, and a solvent like 80% vodka or 95% ethanol. Begin by measuring the dried fennel seeds, using about one part seeds for every five parts solvent, so for example, 100 grams of seeds and 500 grams of solvent. Next, use your cutting tool to chop the seeds finely to release their active compounds. Place the chopped seeds in the glass jar and pour in the solvent, making sure that the seeds are completely covered. Close the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for about two to three weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the solvent extract the active compounds from the seeds. After this time, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Finally, store the resulting Foeniculum vulgare tincture in a cool, dark place and take it as directed by a healthcare professional to help manage excessive sweating.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal tinctures for excessive sweating

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Shogaols are good for excessive sweating because they help to reduce inflammation and improve the body's thermoregulation, which in turn helps to decrease the amount of sweat produced.
Gingerols are good for excessive sweating because they have natural antifungal and antibacterial properties that help to reduce sweat and fight infections that can cause sweating.

Zingiberene is good for excessive sweating because it has natural antifungal and antibacterial properties that help control the growth of yeast and bacteria on the skin, reducing sweat odors and excessive sweat production.
Bisabolol is good for excessive sweating because it has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help soothe and calm the skin, reducing sweating and preventing skin irritations.
Curcumenol is good for excessive sweating because it has antispasmodic properties, which help to relax the nerves and muscles that control sweating, thereby reducing excessive sweat production.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, is a natural remedy that can help reduce excessive sweating. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. These compounds help to calm down the nervous system, which is often responsible for excessive sweating. By reducing the nervous system's response to stress and anxiety, ginger tinctures can help to decrease sweat production. Additionally, ginger has a cooling effect on the body, which can help to reduce heat and body temperature, leading to less sweating. Ginger also has antimicrobial properties, which can help to prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause body odor and skin irritation. Furthermore, ginger tinctures can be absorbed quickly by the body, providing fast relief from excessive sweating.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Foeniculum vulgare herbal tincture that helps with excessive sweating, start by gathering fresh or dried fennel seeds. If using fresh seeds, dry them in a low-temperature oven or by air-drying for a few days. Once the seeds are dry, measure out one part of the dried seeds and combine them with one part of a solvent like 80-proof vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar. The ratio of seeds to solvent is typically one-to-one, but you can adjust it to your liking. Next, label the jar with the date, ingredients, and the phrase 'shake well.' Store the jar in a cool, dark place and let it sit for two to six weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the ingredients blend. This process is called maceration, and it allows the active compounds in the fennel seeds to infuse into the solvent. After the maceration period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids. Your Foeniculum vulgare tincture is now ready to use. You can take a few drops of the tincture under the tongue or add it to water or another liquid, following the instructions of a healthcare professional for the recommended dosage.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal tinctures for excessive sweating

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Valerenic acid is good for excessive sweating because it helps to block the nerve signals that cause sweat glands to work too much, reducing sweat production and giving relief from excessive sweating.
Valeranone is good for excessive sweating because it has anticholinergic properties, which help reduce the activity of sweat glands and thus decrease excessive sweating.

Valerenal is good for excessive sweating because it has anti-sweating properties that help to reduce the activity of the nerves that cause sweating, leading to a decrease in the amount of sweat produced.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis tincture is an herbal remedy that may help with excessive sweating. The main active compound in this tincture is valerenic acid, which has a soothing effect on the nervous system. This can lead to a reduction in stress and anxiety, which are common causes of excessive sweating. When we're stressed or anxious, our body's 'fight or flight' response is triggered, causing our sweat glands to work overtime. By calming the nervous system, Valeriana officinalis tincture may help to reduce the frequency and intensity of these sweat episodes. Additionally, the tincture's sedative properties can promote relaxation and improve sleep quality, which is also linked to reduced sweating. Some people may find it helpful to take Valeriana officinalis tincture at bedtime to tackle excessive sweating during the night, and to use it in the morning to help manage sweating throughout the day.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Foeniculum vulgare herbal tincture that helps with excessive sweating, start by gathering dried or fresh Fennel seeds. Use a clean glass jar to combine 1 part of Fennel seeds with 2-3 parts of high-proof vodka or neutral spirit in a 1:2 ratio. Close the jar tightly and place it in a cool, dark place, shaking it gently every day for 2-3 weeks to help the Fennel seeds infuse into the liquid. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the Fennel seeds. Store the tincture in a cool, dark place and use it as needed, typically taking 20-30 drops in water 2-3 times a day. Keep in mind that tinctures are highly concentrated and should be diluted before consumption. It's also essential to consult with a healthcare professional or a qualified herbalist before using Fennel tincture, especially if you're pregnant, breastfeeding, or have any underlying health conditions.
remedies.image_alt: Valeriana officinalis herbal tinctures for excessive sweating

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Luteolin is good for excessive sweating because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce sweat gland activity and promote overall skin health.
Rosmarinic acid isomer is good for excessive sweating because it has a strong antiperspirant effect that helps to reduce sweat production by inhibiting the activity of sweat glands.

Quercetin is good for excessive sweating because it has antihistamine and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce the production of sweat and calm the nervous system, leading to a decrease in excessive sweating.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, is a natural remedy that can help with excessive sweating. The tincture is made from the leaves and flowers of the plant, which have been used for centuries to calm the nervous system and reduce stress. Excessive sweating, or hyperhidrosis, can be caused by anxiety, stress, and other emotional factors. By calming the nervous system, the Melissa officinalis tincture can help reduce the body's stress response, which in turn can lead to less sweating. Additionally, the tincture has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation that can occur with excessive sweating. When taken internally, the Melissa officinalis tincture can also help regulate the body's temperature and metabolism, which can contribute to excessive sweating. The tincture can be taken in small amounts, usually 20-30 drops, 2-3 times a day, and can be mixed with water or taken sublingually for maximum absorption.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Foeniculum vulgare tincture, start by gathering fresh or dried Fennel seeds. You can purchase these at a health food store or grow your own. Next, combine 1 part Fennel seeds with 2 parts 80% to 100% grain-based vodka in a clean glass jar. The high percentage of alcohol will help extract the herb's active compounds. Fill the jar completely with the liquid, making sure the Fennel seeds are completely submerged. Then, seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for about 2 to 4 weeks. This period allows the Fennel seeds to infuse their properties into the alcohol. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle. Discard the Fennel seeds and reserve the liquid tincture. This can be taken as needed to help manage excessive sweating. It's recommended to start with small amounts and gradually increase as needed.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal tinctures for excessive sweating

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Catechins are good for excessive sweating because they help reduce sweat production by blocking the action of the nerves that stimulate sweating, which can help prevent excessive sweating and provide relief from this condition.

Quercetin is good for excessive sweating because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce stress and anxiety, which can cause excessive sweating, and also has antioxidant properties that help protect the body from damage caused by excessive sweat.

Terpinen-4-ol helps to reduce excessive sweating by having antiseptic and antimicrobial properties that prevent bacterial overgrowth and infections, which can cause sweating, and also by promoting relaxation and calming the nervous system, which can help to decrease sweat production.
Linalool is good for excessive sweating because it has a cooling effect on the body, which helps to reduce body temperature and therefore decrease sweating.

Tannins are good for excessive sweating because they have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help reduce sweat gland activity and prevent skin infections associated with excessive sweating.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree oil, is a natural ingredient often used to help with excessive sweating. The oil is extracted from the leaves of the Melaleuca plant and has been used for centuries for its antifungal and antibacterial properties. When applied to the skin in a tincture form, it can help reduce sweat by killing bacteria that thrive in warm, moist environments. This can help prevent body odor and discomfort caused by excessive sweating. The antiseptic properties of tea tree oil can also help soothe skin irritations and reduce inflammation, making it a popular choice for people looking for a natural solution to excessive sweating. Additionally, tea tree oil can help balance the skin's pH levels, which can become disrupted by excessive sweating, further contributing to its potential benefits. When used in moderation and in combination with other treatments, Melaleuca alternifolia tinctures may be a useful tool for managing excessive sweating.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Foeniculum vulgare herbal tinctures that help with excessive sweating, start by gathering dried Foeniculum vulgare seeds, which are also known as fennel seeds. Then, combine one part of these dried seeds with three parts of a solvent like vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar. Make sure to label the jar with the date and the ingredients used. Next, store the jar in a cool, dark place and let it sit for two to three weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the solvent extract the active ingredients from the fennel seeds. After the extraction period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. This will give you a strong Foeniculum vulgare tincture that can be taken sublingually, which means placing a few drops under the tongue, to help reduce excessive sweating.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for excessive sweating because it has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce stress on the body's sweat glands and improve overall skin health.

Kaempferol is good for excessive sweating because it has antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation, which can help to regulate body temperature and reduce sweating.
Flavonoids (Fennel) are good for excessive sweating because they have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce heat production in the body and improve sweat regulation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata tincture is often used to help people who sweat too much. This is because it has a calming effect on the body. When we're stressed or anxious, our body's 'fight or flight' response kicks in, and we sweat more. Passiflora incarnata helps to calm down this response, so our body doesn't need to sweat as much. It also helps to regulate our body's temperature, which can cause excessive sweating. Some people find that it helps to reduce their heart rate and lower their blood pressure, which can also contribute to excessive sweating. Taking Passiflora incarnata tincture may help to reduce the amount of sweat produced and make you feel more relaxed and calm. It may be especially helpful for people who experience night sweats or hot flashes. The tincture is made from the passionflower plant, which has been used for centuries in herbal medicine. The active ingredients in the plant, such as flavonoids and alkaloids, work together to produce its calming effects.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Foeniculum vulgare herbal tincture, also known as anise or sweet fennel tincture, which helps with excessive sweating, you will need a few basic supplies. First, dry the Foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers by spreading them out in a single layer on a paper bag or a clean cloth, allowing them to air dry completely. Next, combine 1 part of the dried Foeniculum vulgare with 2 parts of a high-proof vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar. The Foeniculum vulgare should be completely covered by the liquid. Store the jar in a cool, dark place and let it steep for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar gently every day or two. After the steeping process, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Your Foeniculum vulgare herbal tincture is now ready to use, typically taken in small amounts, several times a day, mixed with water or another liquid.
remedies.image_alt: Passiflora incarnata herbal tinctures for excessive sweating

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Anethole is good for excessive sweating because it has antifungal and antibacterial properties that help to reduce sweat odor and prevent infections that can cause further irritation.
Coumarins are good for excessive sweating because they have a natural antiperspirant property that helps to reduce sweat and kill bacteria that cause body odor.

Polyphenols are good for excessive sweating because they have antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and soothe the skin, thus potentially decreasing sweat gland activity and minimizing excessive sweating episodes.

Phenolic acids, also known as fennel, are good for excessive sweating because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce the production of sweat glands and also have a cooling effect that helps to reduce body temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare tincture, also known as fennel tincture, is a herbal remedy that can be helpful for excessive sweating. Fennel has been used for centuries to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and anxiety, which are often linked to excessive sweating. The tincture is made from the seeds of the fennel plant, which contain compounds that can help reduce anxiety and calm the nervous system. This can lead to a decrease in the production of sweat, as the body is under less stress. Additionally, fennel has natural antiseptic and antispasmodic properties, which can help soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and irritation caused by excessive sweating. Fennel tincture can be applied topically or taken internally, depending on the individual's needs and preferences. It is essential to note that fennel tincture should be used under the guidance of a healthcare professional, as it can interact with certain medications and have side effects in some individuals.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Foeniculum vulgare herbal tincture for excessive sweating, start by gathering dried fennel seeds, which contain the active compounds that help reduce sweat. Begin by filling a clean glass jar about one-quarter full with the dried seeds. Next, pour a neutral-tasting spirit such as vodka or glycerin over the seeds, making sure that they are completely covered. The general ratio is one part dried herbs to three parts liquid, but this may vary depending on personal preference. Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for about two weeks, shaking the jar every day or two to help the herbs infuse into the liquid. After the two weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids. The resulting tincture can be taken sublingually (under the tongue) as needed, or added to water or tea for a more diluted dose. It's essential to consult a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy, especially if you have any underlying health conditions or are taking medications.

intro_desc: Excessive sweating, also known as hyperhidrosis, can be a frustrating and embarrassing issue that affects many people. Not only can it cause discomfort and anxiety, but it can also lead to skin problems, such as fungal infections and irritation. Using herbal tinctures can be a natural and effective way to address this issue. Avena sativa, also known as oat straw, is a herb that has been used for centuries to calm the nervous system and reduce stress. Its calming properties can help to regulate the body's temperature and reduce excessive sweating. Hypericum perforatum, also known as St. John's Wort, is another herb that has been used to treat anxiety and depression. Its antidepressant properties can help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can be contributing factors to excessive sweating.  Fixing excessive sweating is important because it can improve your quality of life. When you're constantly worrying about sweating, you may avoid social situations, exercise, or even wear certain types of clothing. This can lead to feelings of isolation and frustration. By addressing excessive sweating, you can regain your confidence and start to live a more normal life. You can start to participate in activities that you enjoy, wear the clothes you want, and feel more comfortable in your own skin. Using herbal tinctures like Avena sativa and Hypericum perforatum can be a natural and effective way to achieve this. By reducing stress and anxiety, these herbs can help to calm the body's temperature and reduce excessive sweating.
supplementary_best: For excessive sweating, a combination of Avena sativa, Melissa officinalis, and Foeniculum vulgare is recommended. Avena sativa, also known as oat straw, helps to calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety, which is often a contributor to excessive sweating. Melissa officinalis, or lemon balm, has a soothing effect on the body, reducing stress and promoting relaxation. Foeniculum vulgare, or fennel, has a cooling effect on the body, which can help to reduce sweating.  This combination is effective because Avena sativa helps to calm the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress, while Melissa officinalis promotes relaxation and reduces stress. Foeniculum vulgare then helps to reduce sweating by its cooling effect. Together, these herbs create a balanced approach to treating excessive sweating, addressing both the root causes of the issue and the symptoms themselves.
supplementary_avoid: If you have excessive sweating, you should avoid herbal tinctures that can stimulate your body and make you sweat more. Some herbs that can do this are 'Ephedra sinica' (Ephedra) - it can increase heart rate and body temperature, making you sweat more. 'Yohimbe' (Pausinystalia johimbe) - it's a stimulant that can raise blood pressure and cause excessive sweating. 'Cayenne pepper' (Capsicum annuum) - it can increase circulation and cause your body to heat up, leading to more sweating. 'Ginkgo biloba' - while it can improve circulation, it can also cause your body to sweat more, especially in people with anxiety or stress. 'Yerba mate' (Ilex paraguariensis) - it's a stimulant that can increase heart rate and cause excessive sweating. These herbs can make your symptoms worse, so it's best to avoid them if you have excessive sweating.
supplementary_other_remedies: Apple cider vinegar has been used to help with excessive sweating due to its ability to balance body pH levels. When the body's pH is off, it can cause an increase in sweat production. Drinking apple cider vinegar mixed with water before bed may help reduce sweat at night.  Aloe vera is another natural remedy that can help with excessive sweating. The gel from the aloe vera plant has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm the skin. This can reduce sweat production by reducing stress on the body.  Another remedy is peppermint oil. Peppermint oil can help cool the body down and reduce sweat by improving circulation and reducing inflammation. It can also help reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to excessive sweating.  Bentonite clay is also used to help with excessive sweating. It works by drawing out toxins from the body, which can cause sweat glands to produce more sweat. By removing these toxins, the body's sweat glands can function normally again.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who experience excessive sweating, also known as hyperhidrosis, often have other health issues. One common problem is anxiety or stress, which can trigger the sweat glands to produce more sweat. This is because the nervous system is overactive, sending signals to the sweat glands to release more sweat. Medicinal herbs like ashwagandha and passionflower can help calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety.  Another issue people with hyperhidrosis may face is an imbalance of the thyroid gland, which regulates metabolism and energy production. An underactive thyroid gland, known as hypothyroidism, can cause fatigue, weight gain, and muscle weakness, leading to more sweat. Herbs like bladderwrack and guggul can help stimulate the thyroid gland and improve metabolism.  Excessive sweating can also be linked to digestive problems, such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). IBS can cause stomach pain, bloating, and changes in bowel movements, leading to stress and anxiety. Herbs like peppermint and ginger can help soothe the digestive system and reduce inflammation.  Additionally, people with hyperhidrosis may experience low levels of vitamin B12, which is essential for energy production and nerve function. A deficiency in vitamin B12 can cause fatigue, weakness, and shortness of breath, leading to more sweat. Herbs like alfalfa and spirulina can help increase vitamin B12 levels in the body.  Finally, hyperhidrosis can be associated with adrenal fatigue, a condition where the adrenal glands are overworked and exhausted. This can cause fatigue, weight gain, and insomnia, leading to more sweat. Herbs like licorice root and ashwagandha can help support the adrenal glands and reduce stress.  In these cases, medicinal herbs can help alleviate the underlying health issues that contribute to excessive sweating.
